2604.11628.title: Back to Basics: Let Conversational Agents Remember with Just Retrieval and Generation
2604.11628.paper_summary: Existing conversational memory systems rely on complex hierarchical summarization or reinforcement learning to manage long-term dialogue history, yet remain vulnerable to context dilution as conversations grow. In this work, we offer a different perspective: the primary bottleneck may lie not in memory architecture, but in the \textit{Signal Sparsity Effect} within the latent knowledge manifold. Through controlled experiments, we identify two key phenomena: \textit{Decisive Evidence Sparsity}, where relevant signals become increasingly isolated with longer sessions, leading to sharp degradation in aggregation-based methods; and \textit{Dual-Level Redundancy}, where both inter-session interference and intra-session conversational filler introduce large amounts of non-informative content, hindering effective generation. Motivated by these insights, we propose \method, a minimalist framework that brings conversational memory back to basics, relying solely on retrieval and generation via Turn Isolation Retrieval (TIR) and Query-Driven Pruning (QDP). TIR replaces global aggregation with a max-activation strategy to capture turn-level signals, while QDP removes redundant sessions and conversational filler to construct a compact, high-density evidence set. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that \method achieves robust performance across diverse settings, consistently outperforming strong baselines while maintaining high efficiency in tokens and latency, establishing a new minimalist baseline for conversational memory.
2604.11628.paper_summary_zh: 現有的對話記憶系統依賴於複雜的層級總結或強化學習來管理長期對話歷史，但隨著對話的增長，它們仍然容易受到上下文稀釋的影響。在這項工作中，我們提供了一種不同的視角：主要瓶頸可能不在於記憶架構，而在於潛在知識流形中的\textit{信號稀疏效應}。通過受控實驗，我們識別出兩個關鍵現象：\textit{決定性證據稀疏性}，在這種情況下，隨著會話時間的延長，相關信號變得越來越孤立，導致基於聚合的方法急劇退化；以及\textit{雙層冗餘}，在這種情況下，會話之間的干擾和會話內的填充內容引入了大量非信息性內容，妨礙了有效生成。受到這些見解的啟發，我們提出了\method，一個極簡框架，將對話記憶帶回基礎，僅依賴於通過回合隔離檢索（Turn Isolation Retrieval, TIR）和查詢驅動修剪（Query-Driven Pruning, QDP）的檢索和生成。TIR用最大激活策略取代全局聚合，以捕捉回合級信號，而QDP則移除冗餘會話和對話填充，以構建一個緊湊的高密度證據集。在多個基準上的廣泛實驗表明，\method在多樣化設置中實現了穩健的性能，始終超越強大的基線，同時在標記和延遲方面保持高效率，為對話記憶建立了一個新的極簡基線。
2604.11628.author: Yuqian Wu et.al.
2604.11628.authors: Yuqian Wu, Wei Chen, Zhengjun Huang, Junle Chen, Qingxiang Liu, Kai Wang, Xiaofang Zhou, Yuxuan Liang
